questions.specification:
An engineer is designing a stationary backup power supply for a rural telecommunications tower. The primary requirements are low initial capital expenditure and high reliability, while the total mass of the battery bank is not a design constraint. 

Based on the data provided in the table, which battery chemistry is the most appropriate choice for this specific application?

| Battery Chemistry | Specific Energy ($$Wh/kg$$) | Relative Cost |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Lithium-ion | 150–250 | High |
| Lead-acid | 30–50 | Low |
| Nickel-cadmium | 40–60 | Medium |
| Silver-zinc | 100–130 | Very High |

options.content: Silver-zinc
Lead-acid
Lithium-ion
Nickel-cadmium

questions.specification:
An analysis of a refrigerator's lifecycle results in the following energy consumption data:

| Lifecycle stage | Energy consumption (MJ) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Raw material extraction | 1,450 |
| Processing and manufacturing | 3,250 |
| Transportation to retail outlets | 800 |
| Operational use (10-year lifespan) | 14,000 |
| Recycling and disposal | 500 |

What is the total embodied energy for this refrigerator at the point it is ready for purchase by a consumer?

The table below compares the energy storage characteristics of three different technologies.

| Storage Technology | Specific Energy ($MJ/kg$) | Energy Density ($MJ/L$) |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Compressed Hydrogen | $120.0$ | $1.5$ |
| Lithium-ion Battery | $0.9$ | $2.6$ |
| Lead-acid Battery | $0.1$ | $0.4$ |

Which statement correctly identifies the advantage of using compressed hydrogen over lithium-ion batteries for weight-sensitive applications such as long-range drones?

options.content: It has a much higher specific energy, minimizing the total mass of the energy source.
It has a higher energy density, significantly reducing the volume required for fuel storage.
It operates with a higher round-trip electrical efficiency during the discharge cycle.
It eliminates the requirement for specialized high-pressure containment vessels.

questions.specification:
An engineering team is developing a high-altitude drone for long-range environmental monitoring. The drone's maximum takeoff weight is strictly limited, so the team must select an energy storage system with the highest **specific energy** (energy per unit mass). 

Based on the performance data in the table below, which storage system is the most appropriate choice for this weight-sensitive application?

| Storage System | Specific Energy ($MJ/kg$) | Energy Density ($MJ/L$) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Lead-acid | $0.14$ | $0.32$ |
| Nickel-metal hydride | $0.35$ | $0.48$ |
| Lithium-ion | $0.65$ | $2.10$ |
| Lithium-sulfur | $1.50$ | $1.20$ |

options.content: Lead-acid
Nickel-metal hydride
Lithium-ion
Lithium-sulfur

questions.specification: In the context of utility-scale energy management, why do grid operators engage in 'pumped-storage' operations during periods of low electrical demand?

options.content: To provide the necessary reactive power for voltage stabilization in the transmission network
To store surplus energy for rapid discharge during periods of peak demand
To reduce the environmental impact of thermal pollution in local waterways
To maximize the total annual power generation capacity of the facility

questions.specification: What is the primary benefit of a smart grid system in a large-scale electricity distribution network?

options.content: It increases the physical energy density of electricity within the transmission lines
It allows for the transmission of power over long distances with zero thermal energy loss
It enables the utility company to balance supply and demand through real-time monitoring and data exchange
It converts alternating current (AC) to direct current (DC) without the need for substation transformers

questions.specification:
The table shows the energy requirements for producing one kilogram of various materials from raw (primary) sources and from recycled (secondary) sources.

| Material | Primary production energy ($MJ/kg$) | Recycled production energy ($MJ/kg$) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Aluminium | 160 | 8 |
| Steel | 32 | 16 |
| Glass | 15 | 12 |
| PET Plastic | 80 | 40 |

Based on the data provided, which of the following is a correct conclusion regarding the **embodied energy** of these materials?

options.content: Recycled PET plastic has a lower embodied energy than recycled steel.
options.markscheme: Recycled PET plastic is $40\,MJ/kg$ and recycled steel is $16\,MJ/kg$, so PET plastic is higher, not lower.

options.content: Primary steel has a higher embodied energy than primary PET plastic.
options.markscheme: Primary steel is $32\,MJ/kg$ and primary PET plastic is $80\,MJ/kg$, so steel is lower, not higher.

options.content: The use of recycled aluminium reduces the material's embodied energy by 95% compared to primary production.
options.markscheme: Primary aluminium is $160\,MJ/kg$ and recycled aluminium is $8\,MJ/kg$. Reduction $=\frac{160-8}{160}=\frac{152}{160}=0.95=95\%$.

options.content: Glass requires the most energy for primary production among the materials listed.
options.markscheme: Primary glass is $15\,MJ/kg$, which is not the highest (primary aluminium is $160\,MJ/kg$ and primary PET plastic is $80\,MJ/kg$).

questions.specification:
An engineering firm is designing a lightweight, long-range unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV). The design constraints require a battery system that provides the highest energy storage capacity for the lowest possible mass. 

Based on the performance data in the table below, which battery technology should the team select?

| Battery Technology | Specific Energy ($$Wh/kg$$) | Service Life (Cycles) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Lead-acid | 35 | 400 |
| Nickel-cadmium ($$NiCd$$) | 50 | 1,200 |
| Nickel-metal hydride ($$NiMH$$) | 95 | 700 |
| Lithium-ion ($$Li-ion$$) | 250 | 1,500 |

options.content: Nickel-metal hydride ($$NiMH$$)
options.markscheme: Incorrect. While higher than Lead-acid, it has significantly lower specific energy than Lithium-ion.

options.content: Lead-acid
options.markscheme: Incorrect. Lead-acid has the lowest specific energy in the table, making it the least suitable for a weight-sensitive UAV.

options.content: Lithium-ion ($$Li-ion$$)
options.markscheme: Correct. Lithium-ion batteries have the highest specific energy (250 $$Wh/kg$$), providing the most energy per unit of mass, which is critical for lightweight aerospace applications.

options.content: Nickel-cadmium ($$NiCd$$)
options.markscheme: Incorrect. Although it has a long service life, its specific energy is too low for the primary constraint of high energy storage for low mass.

questions.specification:
An aerospace company is evaluating four different battery technologies for a new drone project. The design requirements prioritize a lightweight power source that can store the maximum amount of energy per kilogram.

| Battery Type | Energy Capacity (MJ) | Total Mass (kg) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Prototype I | 120 | 50 |
| Prototype II | 200 | 80 |
| Prototype III | 150 | 50 |
| Prototype IV | 300 | 120 |

Which prototype offers the highest specific energy?

options.content: Prototype I
options.markscheme: Prototype I has a specific energy of $$120 / 50 = 2.4\text{ MJ/kg}$$, which is lower than Prototype III.

options.content: Prototype II
options.markscheme: Prototype II has a specific energy of $$200 / 80 = 2.5\text{ MJ/kg}$$, which is lower than Prototype III.

options.content: Prototype III
options.markscheme: Specific energy is the energy per unit mass. Prototype III provides the highest ratio: $$150 / 50 = 3.0\text{ MJ/kg}$$.

options.content: Prototype IV
options.markscheme: Prototype IV has a specific energy of $$300 / 120 = 2.5\text{ MJ/kg}$$, which is lower than Prototype III.

questions.specification: How is a product's embodied energy best described?

options.content: The energy consumed during the product's use phase only
The total energy consumed from raw material extraction through manufacturing to disposal/recycling
Only the energy used in manufacturing processes
The energy consumed during raw material extraction and product use
